data.title: J D Wetherspoon: Attractive Valuation and Resilient Market Positioning Support Buy Rating Despite Consumer Headwinds
data.description:
Jefferies analyst James Wheatcroft maintained a Buy rating on J D Wetherspoon today and set a price target of p840.00.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

James Wheatcroft has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors linked to J D Wetherspoon’s competitive positioning and valuation. He views the company as a large-scale operator with a strong value proposition for cost-conscious customers, which should allow it to keep winning market share even as economic pressures persist and some profitability expectations for FY26E and FY27E are trimmed.

At the same time, he judges the stock’s valuation to be attractive, with the shares trading on an enterprise value to EBITDA multiple well below their historical range and supported by a solid free cash flow yield in FY26E. While he acknowledges that worries over the health of the U.K. consumer could cap near-term multiple expansion, he believes the current price already discounts these risks, leaving a favorable risk-reward profile that underpins his Buy recommendation.

Wheatcroft covers the Consumer Cyclical sector, focusing on stocks such as Flutter Entertainment PLC, Mitchells & Butlers, and Entain plc. According to TipRanks, Wheatcroft has an average return of 4.5% and a 46.15% success rate on recommended stocks.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Buy Rating on Optimum Driven by Margin Expansion, Operational Momentum, and Improved Broadband Trends
data.description:
In a report released today, Gregory Williams from TD Cowen maintained a Buy rating on Optimum Communications Inc Class A, with a price target of $2.00.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Gregory Williams has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Optimum’s clear shift toward higher‑margin profitability despite elevated subscriber losses. Management intentionally prioritized average revenue per user and cost efficiency, delivering the strongest EBITDA margin in four years, supported by sizable operating expense reductions and ongoing automation initiatives.

Williams also views the company’s operational momentum as a strategic positive while it navigates competitive headwinds and its bondholder situation. Broadband and video trends are showing measurable improvement versus expectations, and enhanced pricing discipline plus converged service bundles are helping stabilize churn, which he believes sets a more attractive risk‑reward profile at the current valuation.

data.title: BridgeBio: De-Risked Achondroplasia Program and Expanding Late-Stage Rare-Disease Portfolio Underpin Buy Rating and Blockbuster Growth Outlook
data.description:
Jefferies analyst Andrew Tsai maintained a Buy rating on BridgeBio Pharma today and set a price target of $100.00.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Andrew Tsai has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to BridgeBio’s expanding rare-disease portfolio and newly de-risked achondroplasia program. He highlights that Phase III results for infigratinib in achondroplasia show strong, consistent growth benefits with convenient oral dosing and a clean safety profile, supporting a higher probability of success, a raised price target, and over $1B in potential peak sales.

He also underscores that BridgeBio now has four late-stage assets, each with prospective blockbuster-level revenue and clear differentiation versus current standards of care, including Attruby in ATTR-CM, BBP-418 in LGMD2I/R9, and encaleret in ADH1. With multiple planned regulatory filings, anticipated launches from 2026–2027, and management’s strategy to commercialize globally to enhance operating leverage, he believes the company’s long-term growth outlook justifies maintaining a Buy recommendation.

In another report released yesterday, TD Cowen also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $95.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: JBS: Buy Rating Backed by Middle East Expansion, Strategic JVs, and SOTP-Driven Upside
data.description:
Goldman Sachs analyst Thiago Bortoluci has maintained their bullish stance on JBS stock, giving a Buy rating on February 6.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Thiago Bortoluci has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to JBS’s strategic expansion and earnings potential. He highlights that the new joint venture with Oman Food Capital positions JBS as a major regional protein supplier in the Middle East, a market with strong structural growth, attractive margins, and lower geopolitical risk due to food‑security policies.

Bortoluci also underscores that the Middle East hub, together with the partnership with Entaj and the Jeddah expansion, should add meaningful capacity, brand strength, and distribution reach versus peers like BRF. Based on a sum‑of‑the‑parts valuation, he sees upside to JBS’s share price, supported by an attractive 12‑month target and solid NPV contribution from the project, while acknowledging standard industry risks such as FX volatility, supply cycles, and trade disruptions.

In another report released on February 6, UBS also initiated coverage with a Buy rating on the stock with a $19.50 price target.

data.title: Alnylam: ATTR Franchise Momentum and Strategic Investment Support Durable Growth and Buy Rating
data.description:
Analyst Joseph Stringer of Needham maintained a Buy rating on Alnylam Pharma, reducing the price target to $510.00.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Joseph Stringer has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors reflecting Alnylam’s durable growth outlook in the transthyretin-mediated amyloidosis (ATTR) franchise and solid recent execution. He notes that fourth-quarter 2025 product revenue of $995M was in line with prior guidance, and that the company reaffirmed a robust 2026 total product revenue outlook of $4.9–5.3B, with $4.4–4.7B expected from the TTR portfolio, underscoring confidence in long-term demand.

At the same time, he acknowledges higher-than-anticipated 2026 non-GAAP operating expenses of $2.7–2.8B versus consensus, but views this spending as strategic support for growth and market expansion rather than a structural concern. Stringer highlights encouraging early launch metrics for Amvuttra, including payer access and initial share gains in the ATTR-CM segment, which support Alnylam’s ability to defend and expand its position despite competitive pressures, and together these factors justify maintaining a positive Buy recommendation.

In another report released today, Stifel Nicolaus also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $444.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
